reports.title: ApprovalPrep Document Readiness Index
reports.summary: A public, methodology-labeled report that explains the document-readiness signals ApprovalPrep uses to help people prepare truthful application packets.
reports.methodology: This report uses ApprovalPrep-owned route, checklist, template, atom, and source-coverage data. It is an educational readiness index, not a market survey or guarantee of any approval outcome.

sections.heading: What the index measures
sections.body: The index measures whether a document-prep page has a clear user job, a checklist, a self-service boundary, supporting links, and a next step.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: Use the index as a preparation guide: gather real documents, check dates and amounts, separate facts from context, and keep a copy trail.

sections.heading: What it does not claim
sections.body: The index does not measure lender, landlord, employer, or agency approval odds. It does not provide legal, credit repair, financial, or underwriting advice.

reports.title: Apartment Application Prep Report
reports.summary: A public report on the paperwork areas renters should organize before applying for an apartment.
reports.methodology: This report is based on ApprovalPrep rental pages, template previews, and public self-service checklist rules. It is educational and does not claim housing approval outcomes.

sections.heading: Core readiness areas
sections.body: Rental preparation usually starts with identity details, income proof, rental history, explanation letters when needed, and a clear copy trail.

sections.heading: Common packet gaps
sections.body: Applicants often need cleaner dates, consistent address history, income-source clarity, and a short explanation that matches the supporting documents.

sections.heading: Boundary
sections.body: ApprovalPrep helps organize truthful self-service paperwork. It does not contact landlords, screen applicants, create fake documents, or guarantee acceptance.

reports.title: Small Business Funding Document Prep Report
reports.summary: A public report explaining the document-prep areas small businesses can organize before funding conversations.
reports.methodology: This report uses ApprovalPrep business-funding pages and checklist rules. It is not lending advice, underwriting advice, or a financing guarantee.

sections.heading: Document categories
sections.body: Business funding prep often includes ownership facts, revenue proof, tax documents, bank statements, use-of-funds notes, and business context.

sections.heading: Preparation principle
sections.body: A stronger packet separates evidence from explanation and makes the reviewer’s document request easier to follow.

sections.heading: Boundary
sections.body: ApprovalPrep does not submit applications, negotiate terms, create financial records, or promise funding.
